data.title: [test-batched.sh]: parent sessions exit -> orphan bash test-batched.sh processes accumulate across sessions
data.description:
## Expected Behavior

When a Claude Code session exits (closes, crashes, is killed, or its worktree is cleaned up), any `bash test-batched.sh` children it spawned should exit too — either via signal propagation, an EXIT trap in the launching shell, or a session-end cleanup hook. Stale test-batched.sh processes should not accumulate on a developer workstation across days/weeks.

## Actual Behavior

11+ orphan `bash test-batched.sh --runner=node ...` processes observed on this workstation, spanning 11+ days. Most are from worktrees that no longer exist or from Claude agent sub-worktrees that were already cleaned up. All are in state `S` (sleeping, blocked on I/O or a child that never returned) with `0:00.00` CPU time — they spawned, blocked immediately (likely on \`wait $pid` for a node subprocess that died or was reaped by something else), and were never reaped.

Snapshot captured 2026-04-18 21:37:

```
PID    ELAPSED      COMMAND (truncated paths/tmpdirs visible in ps aux)
55059  11-01:13:40  bash .../worktree-20260407-150250/plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh --runner=node --test-dir=/var/.../tmp.HuThY2EeJ5 --timeout=1
37166  10-12:06:03  bash .../worktree-20260407-150250/plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh --runner=node --test-dir=/var/.../tmp.QYQY1LdbbI --timeout=2
43067  08-23:10:06  bash .../worktree-20260409-192221/plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh --runner=node --test-dir=/var/.../tmp.aBCNkdt110 --timeout=2
75138  06-00:04:36  bash .../.claude/worktrees/agent-a5904dd8/plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh --runner=node --test-dir=/var/.../tmp.UEiqYyoZLb --timeout=2
35398  05-03:30:11  bash .../.claude/worktrees/agent-a6b4e5ed/plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh --runner=node --test-dir=/var/.../tmp.dQZevJqd70 --timeout=2
7706   05-01:16:49  bash .../worktree-20260413-164836/plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh --runner=node --test-dir=/var/.../tmp.qfaWM1Abg0 --timeout=1
57397  22:55:28     bash .../.claude/worktrees/agent-adde4050/plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh --runner=node --test-dir=/var/.../tmp.G8uWJw3vQn --timeout=1
43519  05:11:21     bash .../worktree-20260418-155124/plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh --runner=node --test-dir=/var/.../tmp.BripUIEoBF --timeout=2
17422  03:24:59     bash .../digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh --runner=node --test-dir=/var/.../tmp.o5QJxbj4h0 --timeout=2
23863  03:24:53     bash .../digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh --runner=node --test-dir=/var/.../tmp.z2GVwKGex2 --timeout=1
19833  01:37:12     bash .../worktree-20260418-155839/plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh --runner=node --test-dir=/var/.../tmp.5wq9VR7Dp5 --timeout=2
```

## Observations

1. **Every orphan is the bash runner with `--runner=node`.** None of the bash-runner or pytest-runner invocations leaked — only node-runner. This points at node-runner.sh specifically (possibly the node subprocess invocation/wait semantics).
2. **All are short-timeout invocations** (`--timeout=1` or `--timeout=2`). These look like ticket-system-level short-fuse tests (likely test-batched internal self-tests, given the tiny tmpdir names).
3. **All target `/var/folders/z2/.../tmp.XXXXXXXX` directories** that are ephemeral and almost certainly gone now — i.e., the test-dir the runner was scanning no longer exists.
4. **All are in state `S`** with 0 CPU time — classic `wait` on a dead child.
5. **Three separate lineages** of cleanup-missing parents are visible:
   - `worktree-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS/` (dated worktree dirs, some dirs no longer exist)
   - `.claude/worktrees/agent-<hex>/` (Claude sub-agent worktrees that are supposed to be cleaned up when agents finish — `claude-safe` / worktree-cleanup loop)
   - The main `digital-service-orchestra` checkout
6. **hook_cleanup_orphaned_processes** (session-misc-functions.sh) is supposed to kill stale nohup orphans >30 min old at SessionStart — it's not catching these, either because these aren't nohup'd or because the hook's selection predicate doesn't match `test-batched.sh` children.

## Impact

- Process table pollution; new test-batched invocations in this session had to identify-and-kill collisions with the stale ones when session-end cleanup ran.
- Each orphan holds refs to long-deleted tmpdirs; while these are small, they'd survive across laptop restarts only if something holds them (they don't here — they'd die on reboot).
- More importantly: signals that test-batched.sh's own background-child lifecycle (node-runner) has a termination gap — whatever the original parent session was waiting on never returned, and the parent never SIGTERM'd its child before dying itself.

## Reproduction

Difficult to reproduce intentionally — requires a session that invokes `test-batched.sh --runner=node` and then exits uncleanly (e.g., session crash, worktree removal mid-run, Claude Code restart).

Investigation starting points:
1. `plugins/dso/scripts/runners/node-runner.sh` — inspect the `node --test` subprocess handling, particularly how test-batched.sh's main loop waits on node children. Compare to bash-runner.sh which uses `wait $_test_bg_pid` with an explicit PID capture (line ~195 of bash-runner.sh — recently improved per its own comment).
2. `plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh` — check EXIT trap and whether it SIGTERM's child runners before exiting.
3. `plugins/dso/hooks/lib/session-misc-functions.sh::hook_cleanup_orphaned_processes` — selection predicate should include `test-batched.sh` matches, not just nohup-marked processes.
4. Correlate orphan PIDs with their parent Claude session IDs via `ppid` to see whether the parent died cleanly or was SIGKILLED.

## Investigation starting points (alternative — fix the symptom)

Add a periodic orphan-sweeper (cron, launchd LaunchAgent, or SessionStart hook) that targets `bash .*test-batched\.sh.*` processes owned by $USER with >1h elapsed time, in state `S`, with stale or non-existent `--test-dir`. These are reliably orphans.
